title: Purchased through Apple but wanted to leave an honest review here
text:
I bought these through Apple at a discounted price.. got them for around $260. As a frequent Amazon shopper, I wanted to leave a review here because, if you're like me, you rely heavily on Amazon reviews before hitting that "Add to Cart" button on any website. I mostly use these headphones for gaming and listening to EDM music while I work on my computer. They are definitely the nicest pair of headphones I own, I went with the grey and gold version. Overall, I like them but I mostly like them for the $260 price that I bought them for.
I think these are worth buying if you can get them at a discounted price or wait for them to get cheaper!

Pros:
-Aesthetically the most gorgeous pair of headphones I own
-Included a hard shell case, charging cable, wired mode cable
-Battery life lasts long and charges within 10 minutes
-Pairing and device switching is really fast and simple
-Sound quality is good for EDM music
-Range on these is great. I can walk all over my apartment while they are connected to my computer, sound doesn't break up even from behind closed doors

Cons:
-Beats Solo2 headphones are way more comfortable to wear. These are a lot tighter and heavier on my head than my Solo2 headphones so I can't wear them for longer than 3 hours if I'm working at my computer
-Bose 35 noise canceling feature is much better
_gate_reason: The review describes the grey and gold color of the headphones.

title: Definitively improvement over Studio 2.0s
text:
I bought the l Studio 2.0s when they first came out over two years ago. I honestly liked the sound quality and bass (not too overwhelming like first gen Beats) but wish at times I could turn off noise cancellation due to the constant hiss. Noise cancellation was useless especially on a plane. Ear pads on early 2.0s were way too soft and hurt my ears after a while.

Just received Studio 3’s from Apple today and it looks like my complaints were answered. First impressions, noise cancelling is improved but a bit of hit or miss. They removed quite a bit of low end hum from the washing machine running near my bedroom but only marginally removed the noise from the oven kitchen fan (which has a higher pitch noise). Best thing though is I can turn off noise cancelling and get rid of hiss when I’m listening in a quiet environment. Pairing was a cinch and I can vouch for the long range of the bluetooth signal (phone was in upstairs bedroom and I had no signal drop downstairs in the kitchen on the opposite side of the house.

Build quality seems identical to v2.0 and the new matte black v3 looks exactly like my old ones. Cushions on these are thicker and much more comfortable than my old ones. Haven’t used long enough to vouch for battery life but have no reason to believe they won’t go 22 hours w/ noise cancellation, 40 hours without. Sound quality seems to be the same as v2.0. Good mids/highs and strong mid bass (but not deep sub-bass).

I have Bose QC35s and they are tops for noise cancellation. But the Bose bass breaks up if I increase bass levels on EQ just a bit in my Spotify app. No such problem on the Beats, so much more volume headroom as well. If you care more about noise cancellation, go Bose. For sound quality, I actually think the Beats sound better (for reference I also own a set of Bang and Olufsen H7s Bluetooth headphones which I think sound better than both because they don’t make compromises for noise cancellation).
_gate_reason: Describes the matte black color and thicker cushions of the headphones.

title: Not so quiet and not so comfortable
text:
I use this to listen to audiobooks during my long commute to work so noise cancellation and comfort are of utmost importance. I got this as a replacement for my 4 year old Bose QC 15. I like how it looks, but that’s about it.

Noise cancellation is significantly worse than QC 15’s. ANC appears to take a different approach to noise cancellation. It cuts the level of surrounding sound (voice and background noise) equally akin to lowering the volume in half. Whereas the QC 15 is more focused on eliminating background noise — e.g. engine noise, train noise. With ANC on, I still hear significant amount of background noise which makes it hard to understand what I’m listening to. With ANC off, it almost feels like it amplifies the noise around you which makes it unbearable and worse than a pair of Apple EarPods.

The Studio3 is not very comfortable. The clamping force is more than what I would consider comfortable for hours of listening. It’s heavier than the QC 15 so it presses harder against the top of my head. The cup is also not as wide as the Bose. I have normal size ears and the headphones press against my ears partially. All these make me adjust the Studio3 every 15 minutes or so.

In conclusion, I’m quite disappointed with the Studio3. I preordered this so I didn’t get to try them first. I was hoping it would at least be comparable to the Bose QC 35 but sadly that is not the case.
_gate_reason: The review mentions how the headphones look and their comfort features.

title: Useless luxury item in need of validation
text:
I bought these because I thought they would provide a deeper sound and unmatched clarity. Their sleek look is deceptive though; the sound is “sour” in a sense. The bass is not impactful but rather loud. The vocals sound oddly removed and the intricacies of the music disappear. They fit nice and the range is good but the sound is way too simplistic for $350. I would highly recommend the Beats X instead. They are the most durable, complex, and simplistic pair of headphones I have ever owned. They’re also less than half the price and easy to maintain.

The worst part about my purchase was that while I purchased “new” beats studio 3, I received a used return that was clearly evident by the return sticker. Worst still was that it was sold directy by Amazon. My order was not given attention, perhaps under some assumption that I wouldn’t care considering the price It found it at ($220). I thought maybe I had received a fake pair but it turns out the design is just terrible.

Simply put, i was not happy both with the product and how it was sold to me. Watch out for amazon fulfilled items, it’s not an assurance that they’re any better than the upteenth seller on their platform.
_gate_reason: The review mentions the sleek look and terrible design of the headphones.

title: Poor quality less than a year old
text: I was so excited to get these to use for out of town trips!  I don't use these very much only when flying which has been 4 times this year. As you can see in the photos the leather on the ear pieces has craked and torn!  Very dissatisfied with the quality of these concerning the price. They sounded great but now Everytime you wear them black flakes cover your ear from the leather. Less than a year old.
_gate_reason: The review describes the cracked and torn leather on the ear pieces.

title: AMAZING HEADPHONES
text:
[[ASIN:B075G3KM71 Beats Studio3 Wireless Headphones - White]]

I was using these dope headphones while commuting to work. I have used them probably 12 times and haven't had to charge them. I saw a man next to me on the train wearing some janky headphones and I felt bad...Shadow Gray is where its at - extremely comfortable. 10/10 recommend to a friend.
_gate_reason: Describes color and comfort of the headphones.

title: Do not buy these for sound quality.
text: First I'd like to say that I've previously owned the Beats Studio 2.0 and I loved them. Since 2013 that's the only headphones I've used. As far as sound quality goes they've always met my expectations. Which is exactly why I bought the Studio 3.0. I mean it should have the same sound quality with some upgraded features right? Wrong! This has to be up there with some of the absolute worst headphones I've ever owned! The fact that they're only marked down to $279.95 is laughable. Sure they look cool with all their different colors to choose from and they feel pretty nice as far as quality and build goes. But they just don't get loud enough and if you do figure out a way to make them louder, like through Spotify's equalizer feature it only makes them sound worse. I tried this hoping it'd help but all I got was the headphones screeching and popping in my ears!  These are nothing like the Studio 2.0 headphones. My older model is so much louder and can hold up against the loudest songs without the speakers popping in my ears. Stay away from this new model. Apple really messed this one up.
_gate_reason: The review mentions different colors and quality of the headphones' build.

title: Studio³ over Solo³, hands down!
text: I use these headphones whenever I go. This was my first pair of Beats Studio³ headphones and simply bought them for its looks. However, I was so pleased with the noise canceling and the sound quality that I bought the white/gold trim model AND the grey/gold trim model as well. I was expecting the bass to be overwhelming, but it’s actually the perfect amount...and the highs and mids are wonderful as well. I would highly recommend getting the Studio³ model over the Solo³ model because they’re “over ear” headphones versus the “on ear” Solo³ version. I also like that I could rename each one I have so that it’s not confusing when they’re all listed on my devices. I thought I would continue using my Powerbeats³ at the gym...however, I usually only use one of my three Studio³ headphones now.  The Bluetooth really is as seamless as previous reviews have stated and a full charge on the Studio³s are great...they usually last me 2-3 days before having to recharge, and like I’ve explained, I use them pretty often.
_gate_reason: The review mentions the white/gold and grey/gold trim models.

title: Average sound - great noise reduction
text:
Cons:
I was dissapointed by the sound reproduction of this - very lackluster reproduction of highs - treble booster on iPhone helps. The material of the ear cups is very comfortable but will make you feel hot in warm weather. Something as simple as turning them on or off is incredibly frustrating since the button performs many functions - like turning on or off the ANC - so often you will find that you left them on. For phone calls they pick up too much ambient noise. They “feel like they are going to break” where they fold.
Pros:
Bateries last a very long time. The case is convenient. Look great in dark blue. Pairing with iPhone - wow - so easy and it follows you on your devices. Noise reduction is excellent - bought them for air travel and they deliver.
In all I think these are good headphones for traveling. For everyday use - the quality of the sound is not what I expected at this price point. It needs a separate or easier power on/off button (double click maybe?). The case could use a pocket for the cable. You should get the extended warranty.
_gate_reason: The review mentions the headphones look great in dark blue.

title: Do NOT buy Beats now that APPLE owns them
text: I bought a pair of the Beats Studio Wireless earphones and a little over a year later the earphone cushion is falling apart.  It basically is flaking off onto my hair/clothes and just looks very cheap.  I inquired with Apple directly (as that is where APPLE who owns Beats now says you must go for repairs).  Shocking the monopoly/machine basically says - to get new ear cushions for items over 12 months (out of warranty) you need to order a new pair or find something on amazon that is a replacement.  For those who have tried - I went to 2 different websites for replacements and both were not good (ie; didn't fit and were not even close to being the same type of cushion as the originals).  Based on this - my advice is simply to use a different provider.  I am beginning my research now...
_gate_reason: The review mentions the earphone cushion looks cheap and is flaking off.

title: Just buy it
text: I got the studio 3 at around $219USD. For the price, the sound is pleasing and tuned well. Noise cancellation is better than expected. I took the bus while using this and anyone who has been inside a LADOT Dash bus knows that the engine is loud. I was sitting towards the rear and it muffled out most of the noise. I had the studio 2 before and the sound difference is very apparent. Bass is softer than studio 2 but it's done in a very balanced way. The cushion is much softer and more comfortable. Sucks that it still had micro USB but better than it changing to lightning port. Looks identical to studio 2. For me, upgrading was well worth it. Purchased matte black. The quality at this price point is a very good value. Cant justify paying 350 for bose qc II when there's this.
_gate_reason: Describes the product's color, appearance, and comparison to a previous model.

title: Good, but deeply flawed headphone.
text:
I really wanted to like it. But then again, I’ve wanted to like Beats headphones since the first ones were introduced, and I’ve found that even now, nearly a decade into their existence, they still suffer from a lot of the same flaws as they did on day 1.

- Weak, confidence uninspiring plastic. These will not survive regular travel/commuting
- Hinge mechanisms that will make you cringe every time you unfold the headphones. It was very obvious that there was an extremely limited number of times you could fold and unfold these before the hinges would give out. I was unsurprised to find that many long-term reviewers had this issue. Apple - there’s no reason why I should have to buy a new pair of headphones from you every 6 months because they’re so poorly constructed - particularly when they cost $300+!
- The carrying case is inconveniently massive - these headphones should be able to fold down a whole lot better. Apple - see Bose QC headphones for a lesson on how headphone storage should be done.

There are some positives, but they come with caveats as well.

- Sound quality is good for a Bluetooth headphone, which is to say that it’s totally uninspiring by any standard set with wired phones. They have better bass than a pair of QC-25s, but they have much less of a soundstage, which often leaves them sounding a bit muddy.
- I have a pair of 15 year old Koss Porta-Pros that sound better than these, and they were probably $50? Yeah, the technology has a way to go.
- W1 chip - the headphones sync up quickly with an i-device, but no more so than any other wireless product made by any of the leading competitors. Not sure why people keep cheering about this aspect.
- ANC noise cancellation - is better than previous iterations of this headphone, and yet still not on par with even the Bose QC headphones of yesteryear.
- Design - kinda cool... in a 2000s sort of way. It’s 2018, maybe time for a refresh?

In summary, these will be a great buy if Apple ever comes to it’s senses, and prices these “not terrible, not great” headphones closer to the $150-200 range.
_gate_reason: The review mentions the design being 'kinda cool' and suggests a refresh.

title: Great headphones for a college student
text:
Beats by Dre are definitely the kind of headphones people buy because of the name on them. Although I don’t particularly think there is anything wrong with these, my first impression is that for half of the money you can get a decent pair of headphones.

With that said, the noise cancelling of these is fantastic. It works wonderfully for a college student like myself who wants to have music playing softly while drowing out some of the background noise. The sound quality itself is good. I say good and not great because a lot of headphones in the $100 price range can match the sound quality, but usually without features like bluetooth and noise cancelling. The battery life is more than adequate for myself and I really don’t have anything seriously bad to say about them.

The only tips I have are to not buy these for working out. If you train hard like I do, you start getting hot, sweating, etc. Bigger headphones like these will trap the sweat around your ears and actually make you feel hotter. Because I bought them in white, I would not recommend them also for the fact they may stain yellow if worn to the gym often.

Overall great pair of headphones and fit my needs well. The features seem to meet the price well. I used to think beats were all extremely overpriced, but considering these have more features and quality than they ever used to for even less than what they used to cost when they came out, the company has improved greatly on this.

EDIT: It has now been nearly a year and they have not degraded in any way. They’ve been durable and maintained good function. Very satisfied with their quality.
_gate_reason: The review mentions the headphones are white and may stain yellow.

title: Disappointed

title: The best sounding headphones I have ever used.
text:
Honestly, the best sounding headphones i have ever heard. While these do not produce a "flat" sound like most studio monitors (like Sony MDR-7506, they sound spectacular. I listen to music on headphones about 4-6 hours a day and these have completely blown me away. I no longer use my skullcandy earbuds, my Sony, Vmodas, or Audio Technicas (see below). I love these headphones.

Pros
- incredible sound. The bass is punchy, filled with depth. Mids and highs sound great as well. While they don't have a truely "flat response", they sound excellent. If you listen to Rap, EDM, Metal, Punk, Jazz, Rock they sound insane.
- Extremely comfortable. The ear pads are plush and conform around your ear. I can wear them for hours with zero fatigue.
- Battery life: Insane, lasts me for 3 days (avg listening 6-8 hours) before needing to recharge. They recharge to 100% very quickly (within about 2 hours), they also have a quick charge feature where they can get several hours of playback on a few hours charge)
- Bluetooth range: this is the crazy part, I can leave these on one end of my gym and walk about 130 feet away and get full signal even through two.
walls.
- Durability: Headband is metal with solid hinges. They feel sturdy.
- Looks: they look badass. I got them in matte black and love them.
CONS:
- Price: they can be rather expensive to some, but honestly for the quality you get I actually think they worth it.

I have some Sony MDR-7506(s), Audo Technica ATH-M50x, and V-Moda M-100's and these sound MUCH, MUCH better. In fact, I no longer use ANY of the other ones. If these break, I'm buying another pair. Period.
_gate_reason: The review describes the headphones' color, material, and overall appearance.

title: Cheap headphones that are well advertised
text: I got these headphones last year from Apple as part of a promotional.  I still paid 275, but Apple considered that a promotional.  I just opened them today.  These are cheap headphones that are well advertised.  Just because a product gets high visibility,doesn't mean its good.  The product feels cheap.  It doesn't set snug on your head.  Instead they fall off my head.  When I extend the headphones they flop around and don't hold the extension setting.  I have no idea what the sound quality is, because I didn't bother.  What really irritates me is that they are packaged as though they were an after thought by Apple.  They don't contain the proper chords for my Iphone 8.  They don't work with my Apple charger.  They also don't contain a charger in the box, which is horrible for a product that has to be charged and costs $300 plus dollars.  They fold up in a very flimsy manner.  They are tiny and barely cover the ears of an adult male.  They look stupid on the head.  Its like people who wear skinny jeans.  It looks like you are wearing your kids jeans, but they are for grown-ups.  This product is junk.  I feel ripped off.
_gate_reason: The review describes the headphones as tiny and barely covering ears.

The battery life is long enough to cover an international flight! I find a single charge enough for a week's normal use.

CONS: the outside finish doesn't look as good as the picture. After some while you will notice wears on the headphone.

title: Call quality is garbage.
text:
The call quality is trash. Like the music is great and the headphones themselves are stylish. I gave it 4 stars as if they were just for listening to music I wouldn’t have minded but for $350.00 I have had people say they couldn’t hear me clearly during a phone call in my quiet house let’s not even get started on talking in the real world. This is a huge deal breaker for me because it’s annoying that my $19 wired earphones lets me speak crystal clear on calls and it’s irritating as hell to have to look for them every time i want to pick up a handsfree call. maybe it’s the microphone but idk I’m just really annoyed by how terrible that feature has been especially seeing how much I wished to use it. Also they’re cracked on the right strap that’s attached to the earcup. I’m not overly rough or anything this is just from daily use and standard wear and tear. I’ve had them for less than a year. The entire thing is plastic and dosent seem put together all that sturdy to be honest. The Apple chip and easy connectivity sold me on these but I’m regretting my purchase as it’s like $200.00 to fix them through Apple 😑.

I wouldn’t recommend them.

———————
Update Jan 10,2020

I’m reducing my rating to 2 stars. I fixed them but now both sides have cracked again...After paying to fix them once I won’t be doing it again. These headphones are trash and I HIGHLY suggest you save your money.
_gate_reason: The review mentions cracks on the right strap and overall plastic material.

title: Convenient+cool, but mediocre sound and noise canceling, and crappy pleather ear pads
text:
They're super convenient – it's very easy to pair and switch between all your Apple devices. And they definitely look cooler than just about any other headphones out there. I also find them super comfy and can wear them for hours at a time.

The sound is just 'ok' – definitely not comparable to the Sonys or other headphones I've heard in the same price range. You can improve it with an equalizer, but the upper range is pretty bland sounding no matter what you do.

The noise cancelling is pretty bad. It does block out a decent amount of airplane noise, but you can still hear people talking, street noises, etc, and the microphones pick up any slight breeze and pipe it in. You'll definitely get a lot of wind noise with ANC on.

In terms of materials quality, everything looks nice. I really like the choice of plastic for the body – I got matte black. HOWEVER, the ear pads are total crap. They're some low-tier pleather material and started degrading at exactly 1 year. By the time it started really falling apart, getting black flakes everywhere it was out of warranty. I should have contacted support slightly sooner I guess, but you would expect $350 headphones to last more than 1 year without falling apart. Totally unacceptable. And Apple wasn't willing to budge on the warranty even after 3 hours on the phone.

I got mine on sale for $200 – decent value for that price. For anywhere around $250+ consider going with something with better sound and durability unless you're dead set on convenience and style.
_gate_reason: The review describes the matte black color and pleather ear pads.

title: This is a Steal at $215
text: I already own a pair of Studio2s, so I know what to expect performance wise. I was a bit unsure as to whether or not I wanted to buy these, because the price was just so good. But I’ll let you in on a little secret, my guys. These headphones are seriously legit. The packaging is all factory sealed and everything is included in the box. title: If it was under $200 it would have a higher rating.
text:
It's a good headphone. Wouldn't recommend it to anyone though....

Pros:
- Extremely Balanced sound, It's honestly comparable to my ATH-M50 which is what I use to mix music professionally. I was surprised by that.
- Long battery life
- Looks fantastic, and the beats logo isn't obvious
- Comes with a nice hardish case

Cons:
- Must have battery when using it wired
- Uncomfortable, the padding isn't quite stiff enough so your ear ends up directly on the plastic which becomes painful after about an hour of use
- Noise Cancelling is subpar when compared to competitors

For the price I wouldn't recommend these. They're shockingly uncomfortable. I'm going to try different ear pads and maybe that'll fix the issue but you're better off spending a little more money and getting the QC35.
_gate_reason: The review mentions the headphones' fantastic look and discreet logo.

title: Slowly won over with Beats by Dre!
text:
I've been eyeing these headphones for quite some time and didn't know if I should go for it. I actually own a pair of Beats X, which I love but didn't know if I actually needed another pair of headphones or earphones. So no, I didn't need a pair of Beats Studio3, but I'm pretty happy I bought them. When Beats as a brand first came out, I wrote them off as an expensive, pretty piece of gear that was more about looks and less about the product. I've been on a (earbud) kick for the last 4 or 5 years finding amazing performance through various top of the line products. I always liked the natural noise canceling by the earbud sealing into your ear canal. Anyways, about a year and a half ago, I was looking for a new pair of headphones after using the jaybirds for a while which were good but just good. If you win me over the first time, I'll give you the benefit of the doubt and support your products. Because of reviews and that Beats by Dre is an Apple company, I bought the Beats X. Because of the excellent performance and joy of the Beats X, I knew the Beats products were more than just image, so I bought the Beats Studio3.

Fast forward to yesterday when I decided to pull the trigger on the Beats Studio 3. I unboxed, turned them on, and put them on. They are super comfortable, and I didn't even play any music yet, but it was so quiet. I travel for work, and was in a crappy hotel with a noisy AC, plus my room was close to the elevator. I was surprised with the noise canceling. No white noise, just what felt like natural silence. I sat back and listened to music for a couple hours and the battery only went down to 89%. I really enjoy these suckers and am very happy I made the purchase. Of course my Beats X will still go in my pocket whenever I walk out the door, but when I'm working, or traveling, these headphones are now essential and will always be in my backpack. Lastly, I used to always want to buy an expensive pair of Bose noise canceling headphones that I always see the rich old guys wearing on the airplanes. But I think I'm good, I'm pretty happy and think I have one of the better products available on the market.

PS. I bought mine from the local Target because I didn't want to wait until I got home to buy off of Amazon. I bought the white/gold. I was a little confused that the shadow grey/gold was $100 more. Just for the color?
_gate_reason: The review mentions the color white/gold of the headphones.

title: Uncomfortable, Great Sound
text:
The Pros:
- Overall excellent sound quality. I’ve owned Bose and Panasonic noise-canceling headphones in the past, and these are definitely the best in this area.
- Very easy to set up a Bluetooth connection to my iPhone 7 Plus. Simply couldn’t have been easier. All I had to do was turn the headphones on with my unlocked phone nearby. Done.
- Excellent battery life at approximately 22 hours, although I didn’t get to actually test this out. The latest similar Bose headphones claim 20 hours.
- Very slick appearance. I’m a big fan of matte black.

The Cons:
- The headphones (the part that you actually put over your ears) are far too small, both in circumference and depth. Neither my girlfriend nor I could easily fit them over our ears. I tried wearing the headphones for an extended period of time just to see how they’d feel, and I couldn’t wear them for more than 20 minutes without noticeable pain. For this reason, I returned these headphones.
- The headphone case was quite a letdown. It has no internal or external pockets to store your charger, backup stereo aux cable, etc. I bought my first pair of high-end noise-cancelling headphones in 2008, and that case had plenty of such pockets. Come on, Beats, we expect better than this. Such a silly oversight.
- No iPhone companion app (that I’m aware of). As a comparison, Bose has an amazing companion app that lets you make sound quality adjustments that you can’t make by interacting with the headphones directly. It also allows you to easily download firmware updates to your headphones.

Summary: Do not buy these headphones. You’ll wish you didn’t.
_gate_reason: The review mentions a slick matte black appearance of the headphones.

title: Great sound quality, terrible material quality.
text: First things first, the sound quality is very nice and the overall feel of the headphones is very comfortable. Battery life is long lasting and doesn’t take long to charge. Visual design is appealing and it just looks cool. But unfortunately these headphones suffer from terrible quality in regards to the sturdiness. I’ve had to replace the headband Twice within the span of a year because it just keeps snapping. These headphones degrade quickly with normal use, which should not be the case because of the price tag. Even now my headband is starting to break AGAIN and the earmuffs are starting to peel. It’s such a shame because the sound quality is so good and I want to use them more, but I cannot recommend these headphones because they should not take this much maintenance.
_gate_reason: The review mentions the visual design and appealing look of the headphones.

title: Great battery life, decent sound, questionable build quality
text:
I received these as an xmas gift. For reference I am comparing with a pair of OG 2011 beats studio pros and MS Surface headphones from 2020.

The battery life is the best I have and amazing.

The sound is okay. Compared to the 2011 pros and surface, they are a distant third. They seem to my old ears fairly flat EQ wise, which is surprising for beats. All beats I've owned previously, especially the 2011's are extremely bass heavy. These aren't, they are indeed seeming flat response studio headphones.

Build quality. Okay, these are really questionable here. I feel like they won't last. I guess we will see. They do seem to be a metal headband wrapped in plastic. I am concerned with the hinges though as they seem plastic from there down.

The onboard controls are okay. It's usually hard to get your bearings on what you are touching and I usually hit the stop instead of volume. The surface phones are the best controls period. It would be nice if these studios has something to differentiate which control you're on.

Padding. The headband padding is okay. Nothing substantial but comfortable. The ear holes are not large. I have gigantic gauged dumbo ears, so I am a bad reference. But the ear phone is like the headband, thin but still comfortable. At this price point I feel that should be more substantial since they are "studio" and designed for long wear.

Electronics. You can't tell when you power then on, if you are wearing them. There is an audible tone when you connect via BT though. One cool feature is they auto power on when you connect the audio cable.

All in all, I wouldn't recommend as a first choice purchase, but still okay device. Don't pay retail, they are definitely NOT worth that.

HTH
_gate_reason: Describes materials and build quality of the headphones, including metal and plastic.

title: Good product; NOT great for public transit and causes ear pain
text:
So I didn’t want to give this product 1 star, because it’s not really the product’s fault. I bought these because I ride public transit 2+ hours each day. It’s loud and with my AirPods, I have to turn the volume near max to hear most podcasts. I wanted to protect my hearing and also hear what I’m listening to.
First impression, I love the look of these headphones and when I used them at home, amazing music quality.
First day on the bus: when there is a change in the environment, the headphones adapt to the louder sound. But when the bus hits a pothole, it lasts about .5 seconds. This results in many loud “reactions” from the noise canceling feature of the headphones. I had to turn off the noise canceling to even use them on the bus because it was painful.
After about 2 days, I noticed intense ear and jaw pain. I brushed it off and thought it was something else. A little research told me that this happens to a lot of people.

Unfortunately, I’ll be returning these.
_gate_reason: The review mentions loving the look of the headphones.

title: Worst designed product I've ever owned. Breaks repeatedly after 1-2 years of normal use.
text:
This photo is of Apple's flagship pair of headphones, the $350 Studio 3 Wireless, after 2 years of normal use, believe it or not. A terrible product. I have four other photos showing major defects in the cord, the earpieces, and the other side of the headband, but for some reason, Amazon won't let me upload more than 1 photo without errors today.

Just putting them on and off or your head, without applying any pressure to the band, causes the plastic to snap​ in two. This first happened a year ago. I called Apple support and they told me this is "normal wear and tear," to be expected, that they could repair for $200. Normal wear and tear? Really? For a 1-year old product with normal use? I should expect to pay $200 each year for a "repair"? So I superglued it back together instead, until a few months later the other side snapped. Superglued both sides as often as I could until it became impossible. The damaged cord, by the way, was lightly used and never left my house. This​ is the most terribly-designed product I've ever owned.
_gate_reason: The review describes visible defects in the headphones' cord and earpieces.

title: Fits my needs just fine

The build of these headphones are high quality and they weigh little to nothing. The ergonomics feel good and I have no issues with discomfort. Noise cancellation is pretty good, actually. Beats battery is actually pretty good. I can go about 4 - 5 days without having to charge my Beats. All in all, I recommend these headphones. They are good for what I need them for.

title: Amazing Purchase
text: First off, this review is by someone who had always used earbuds, and one pair of Skullcady Hesh 2 Wireless Headphones. This product is definitely a step up from both of those, but that is expected considering the price. But, I believe these are worth the high price. The sound is very good for casual music listening and Netflix/YouTube. I am not a fan of huge bass when listening to music, and the bass on these are not too crazy. The noice cancellation is great, but I personally rarely use it. It kills the battery much faster than when it is off(when off it lasts the 40 hours advertised, when on it only lasts 3-4 hours). It definitely shouldn’t be used when there isn’t much sound to cancel. Without the noice cancellation on, the headphones still do a good job of keeping the sound from leaking so others can hear you, and keeping you from hearing things around you. You can still hear your surroundings, but not as well. The battery outside of using the noice cancellation is amazing. I charge the headphones once or twice a week, and I use them a lot. Not only that, it only takes about 2 hours to charge from 0%! The headphones also look great, which you expect when looking at Beats, as they are a status symbol when it comes to headphones. Finally, I’ll speak on the durability. I’ve had them since November(it is February when I am writing this so I may update this in the future) and use them often, yet I have had no issues with anything breaking or malfunctioning. The closest thing to that is sometimes the headphones will be on but stop playing sound, and to fix it I just have to turn them off and back on. However, this has happened twice in the countless hours I’ve been using them. I’d recommend waiting for them to go on sale for around $280 since they are on sale for that price somewhat often. I got them for that price and am estatic with that decision. I only wish I decided to make the purchase sooner(I started considering getting these in June of last year, I just take a loooong time making expensive decisions).
_gate_reason: The headphones look great and are a status symbol when it comes to headphones.

title: Mediocre sound, but comfortable, and my kid loves them
text: These headphones are not for anyone who appreciates good audio quality. Well, that's a given, really. If you are considering buying these, or Bose, or similar, chances are you are not an audiophile... My 12yo son wanted comfortable over-the-ear headphones that help him submerge in his world of games, videos, and music for a while.  These feel very comfortable, easily pair with Apple devices, and look nice. To me, the sound is just awful (compared to the high-end Sennheisers I have), they sound almost muffled and flat. But noise cancelling and overall comfort are good, and, I suppose they are fine if you are not too picky about the sound quality. Works for my kid, he's happy. But if you are looking for more quality for your money, these headphones are certainly not worth the price.

title: Okay While it Lasted, Quality of Product not desirable.
text:
I'll start off by saying I've only owned a pair of quality, wireless headphones and they both have been manufactured by Beats.

For the first six months of use (20+ hrs of weekly use), these particular headphones worked very well. Sound quality was above average and very clear and the over ear fit was comfortable, although wearing it for 2+ hours at a time sometimes caused me some irritation. I work in a very quiet environment and was never told of any excessive noise coming from my desk due to these headphones. They neatly fold up in the provided case and the charger has worked perfectly since purchase. Audio male to male cord also worked as expected. Also, the microphone in the headset is an added bonus that has helped from time to time but isn't a feature that I necessarily looked for.

The negatives that I have had with this and other Beats products that I have purchased are their durability in the medium-to-long term after purchase. This pair of headphones has shown significant deterioration on the ear pads and pieces of the leather-like surface have started to flake off of the pad. The pad is still soft and comfortable, however, it is not what was expected when I purchased the product.

Lastly, and perhaps the most significant, is that each set of Beats headphones that I have purchased has, without fail, had a rupture in the headband and has resulted in either continuous monitoring to ensure it doesn't break any further or it has rendered the product useless as it will not set over my ears as intended.

Overall, I think the product is okay for the occasional user (2-5 hours per week) that treats it meticulously well, however, anybody looking to use this long-term should significantly consider an alternative unless you expect to change headphones every six months or so.
_gate_reason: The review describes deterioration of ear pads and flaking leather-like surface.

Sound of music gets horrible when the noise cancelling starts acting up. Using these on a plane is painful. Better to just turn noise cancelling off and turn up music to drown out the plane. Back to the drawing board beats. Don't offer noise cancelling if it don't work.

title: I feel like apple went backwards in sound quality
text:
I’m a huge fan of apple and beats. I was a fan of beats before apple bought them out. I owned a pair of the orginal studios and they were amazing headphones I compared the quality to the bose and didn’t see much difference. I favored bose instantly because of the look.

I just bought a new pair of the studio3’s, because after 2 years the battery went bad in my origami studios. Well the style and look and obviously improved. On the other hand, the bass quality was honestly much better when monster owned beats. The crisp, clear sound is still there but the bass is just not the same. I also have to consider I haven’t broken these headphones in I’ve only had them for about 24 hours. Breaking in any kind of speaker can make a world of difference. Usually specifically for base. One thing I also noticed is they are much more comfortable I can, and have already worn these for several hours at a time with no soreness or discomfort.

One thing I might add, apple is taking much credit for improving Bluetooth. That’s a great improvement, but at the same time they should be able to put the spotlight on the sound quality. Is Bluetooth that important of a feature that they focus all their time on it?

People often tend to complain about the poor durability of these headphones. I have an extremely strong feeling than many people don’t realize these headphones are meant to be tossed around and just thrown in the bottom of your bag. If you take care of these headphones they will last. I got 2 years out of my older beats with no problems. Take care of your headphones and they will last. Otherwise these headphones are great, if you are looking for great quality, comfort and willing to take care of your headphones, than these are great investment.
_gate_reason: The review describes the style and look of the headphones.

title: and absolutely loved them. The reason I switched to the Studios ...
text:
Beats Studio Wireless 3:
Recently I got a pair of these headphones for Christmas. I have had Beats in the past, such as the Powerbeats 2, and absolutely loved them.

The reason I switched to the Studios is because I wanted a pair of headphones that are:
1- Noise cancelling
2- Have a good bass
3- Have a high volume
The Studios include all of these features which make them a great set of headphones for me. Every time I work out I listen to music, its a necessity for me to get a good workout in. The weight room is generally always really loud so the noise cancelling feature is nice to have. Before, when I had the Powerbeats, I did not get this "noise cancelling" experience which always made me turn the volume up extremely loud so I didn't have to hear the noise of weights slamming around. Consequently this eventually made the volume not sound so loud to me after a while. The combination of the noise cancelling and high volume feature go together very nicely to constantly block out of noises and make the music sound great, on the Studios.

When you see these headphones, I'm sure that most people think that they would be too heavy to be comfortable in while working out. This was my initial thought too before I tried them on and found out that in fact they are very light and comfortable. If you go from the Powerbeats to the Studios such as I did it might take you a little while to get used to the size of them as well as the feeling of the ear pads.

Just like everything, however, they do have a few flaws to them.

Beats are notorious for trying to make their products as cheap as possible, and sell them at a very high price. As you can see, these headphones are right around $350, which makes them a risky purchase. Like I mentioned before they are a very light set of headphones, which comes at a cost. The material that makes the beats seem to feel a little cheap and as if they could break kind a easily. I just make sure to always have them in a case and be sure not to drop them.

All around I love using these headphones and would recommend them to anyone who is looking for an upgrade and willing to spend the money.
_gate_reason: The review describes the headphones' light weight and comfort during use.
